tax: A pathway can be taxological in the extended catalogue sense even when it has no independent body. Rank and place it by what it reliably changes, transmits, or produces.
ap: The pathway’s field signs are usually effects on the host: altered teeth, shadows, scars, eyeshine, hybrid bones, moon-cycle symptoms, glamour marks, planar seams, or negative-energy residue.
eco: Describe transmission ecology rather than diet: bites, burial failures, oaths, relic exposure, planar breaches, inherited curses, bargains, corpse conditions, seasonal triggers, or Nadir pressure.
beh: Behavioral effects should be written as pressures on the host: hunger, territorial compulsion, grief loops, pack instinct, glamour etiquette, contract reflexes, shadow avoidance, or memory distortion.
traitsText: Separate original species traits from pathway traits so transformed dwarves, grey-folk, troglodytes, beasts, and fey-touched bodies do not collapse into one generic category.
abilities: Only stable pathway powers belong here: hybridization, reanimation, possession, planar resistance, glamour, regeneration, curse spread, or other repeated effects.
bg: This guide prevents transformed beings from being filed as ordinary descendants of the host or the thing they resemble.
g: This guide explains branches that alter organisms without being ordinary organisms themselves: curses, infections, planar pressures, divine scars, and other host-rewriting pathways.
